summary: Re-architect xlsxdiff from a flat Markdown change-list emitter into a deterministic JSON fact-graph (the single source of truth) plus a two-stage skill: (1) code produces ground-truth facts with stable ids, units, roles, headline metrics and confidence; (2) an agent writes a grounded narrative that may only CITE fact ids and does zero arithmetic, gated by a mechanical verifier that rejects any number not present in the JSON. The governing principle is "a plain-but-correct diff beats a rich-but-wrong one": ship the correctness fixes that protect the headline from noise (1D column-alignment pass, number-format/unit capture, sheet-rename, robustness/determinism), ship the full grounding apparatus (JSON + verifier + citation contract + role/headline extraction + materiality), but treat the two genuinely dangerous features explicitly. Column alignment ships as INDEPENDENT 1D passes (must) with a row-only fallback on low column confidence; joint/iterative 2D fixed-point is DEFERRED. A causal dependency graph ships as a SHOULD but every edge is confidence-tagged and the narrative must hedge ("likely drove", never "caused") unless the path is proven; the EBITDA bridge/waterfall and full cross-sheet ref-remap are DEFERRED because a confidently-wrong causal/contribution number is exactly the hallucination the design exists to kill. Schema is versioned (schema_version + diff_run_id + sha256) so the verifier can refuse cross-run contamination. GM acceptance: 16,300 naive changes collapse to ~6 moves/6 inserts/4 deletes/20 inputs and headline_metrics surface Revenue +5.5% / EBITDA +12% / margin 8.06%->8.55% with traceable source_fact_ids. Key files: bizconnect/xlsxdiff.py (engine), bizconnect/connectors/xlsx.py (CLI), skills/workbook-diff/SKILL.md (orchestration), plus new bizconnect/xlsxverify.py (verifier).

features.name: Deterministic JSON emitter with stable fact ids
features.spec: Add emit_json(wd)->dict + diff_to_json(); CLI --json OUT and --format json|md|both. Serialize the FULL WorkbookDiff UNCAPPED: every move/insert/delete/input/value/formula becomes an atomic fact with a stable id (Fnnnn), tier (cause|effect), type, old/new addrs, old_raw+old_display+new_raw+new_display, number_format, unit_kind, role, materiality, and cause/effect links. Header carries schema_version, diff_run_id (uuid+timestamp), engine_version, old/new sha256, status, diagnostics, totals, routing. This is the single source of truth; diff.md stays the capped human preview. The agent grounds on diff.json ONLY and never re-parses diff.md.
features.approach: New to_dict on SheetDiff/WorkbookDiff plus a flat-fact flattener. Assign ids deterministically: sort all collections by (sheet, type, addr/row) BEFORE id assignment; sequential Fnnnn fallback. json.dumps(sort_keys=False, ensure_ascii=True). Golden-file test asserts compare(A,B) JSON is byte-identical across runs.
features.risk: Low technical, but LOAD-BEARING: id instability across runs => verifier false-fails. Mitigate with documented deterministic sort + byte-stability golden test. Schema must be versioned so verifier/skill don't silently break.

features.name: Number-format / unit capture + display rendering
features.spec: In the loader capture cell.number_format alongside value. Derive a semantic unit_kind in {percent, currency, scaled, integer, ratio, date, text, unknown}, currency symbol, and display scale (k/m, $'000 headers). Emit BOTH old_raw (exact, for delta math + verifier) and old_display (format-applied, for prose) on every fact and headline. Code computes delta_abs/delta_pct/delta_display (percents in pp, currency in display scale). Unknown formats fall back to raw with unit_kind=unknown — NEVER assert a wrong unit.
features.approach: read_only=True still exposes cell.number_format, so extend _sheet_cells to store (value, number_format) or a parallel format map. fmt_value(raw, kind) renderer. Classify number_format string with a conservative ruleset; record format_coverage in diagnostics. Lazy/per-fact format read keeps cost low on big models.
features.risk: Medium. Custom/locale formats are a long tail; wrong-unit rendering (margin as $) is worse than no unit, so degrade to unknown+raw. read_only format access must be verified on real models.

features.name: Column-alignment pass (1D, symmetric to rows)
features.spec: Add a second SequenceMatcher over COLUMNS. Column signature = (header-row label for that column, tuple of rounded cached values down stable anchor rows). Recover inserted/deleted/moved columns as first-class facts. CRITICALLY make the ROW signature column-shift-invariant (label + sorted multiset of rounded values WITHOUT raw column position) so a column insert no longer corrupts row alignment. Diff cells on the (aligned_row, aligned_col) lattice. Without this, one inserted period regenerates the 16,300-noise in the column axis.
features.approach: Run the two 1D passes INDEPENDENTLY (not jointly): align rows seeded on column-A labels, align columns seeded on header-row labels. Emit row_confidence and col_confidence. On LOW col_confidence, FALL BACK to row-only comparison and emit LOW_COL_CONFIDENCE warning rather than asserting wrong column structure. If even reliable column re-lattice is hard to land, demote to 'detect+report inserted columns but do NOT re-lattice' — still kills the spurious flood.
features.risk: Medium-high. A bad column anchor mis-aligns a whole sheet and the narrative faithfully misreports it. Mitigated by anchor-on-text-labels-only + col_confidence + row-only fallback. Joint 2D is deferred precisely to keep this safe.

features.name: Row-role classification (input/intermediate/total/output)
features.spec: Per-row classifier exposed on every fact: role in {input, driver, intermediate, total, output, header, spacer} + role_confidence. Signals: label lexicon (revenue/sales, COGS, gross profit, EBITDA, EBIT, opex, net income, margin, total, subtotal); formula shape (SUM/SUBTOTAL over a contiguous range => total); dependency fan-out from the graph (high out-degree => driver, high in-degree+named => output); number format (percent/bold => margin/total).
features.approach: Configurable lexicon (regex). Require corroborating formula-shape or graph evidence before an 'output' tag is allowed to drive a headline. Carry confidence so the agent hedges and a mislabel degrades to omission not assertion.
features.risk: Medium. Locale/naming-sensitive; a mislabelled total promotes a wrong headline. Mitigated by corroboration requirement + confidence + closed citable set (a missed headline is a safe omission).

features.name: Headline-metric extraction + ranking
features.spec: From high-confidence output/total rows pick a deterministic as-of column (rightmost numeric, or header matching Total/FY/sum) and emit ordered headline_metrics: {label, role, unit_kind, old_raw, new_raw, delta_abs, delta_pct OR delta_pp, delta_display, source_fact_id, driven_by, confidence, as_of_col_header}. This is what surfaces Revenue +5.5% / EBITDA +12% / margin 8.06%->8.55% out of the ripple — the closed set the exec summary may cite.
features.approach: Deterministic as-of selection; expose as_of_col_header so the agent/verifier can sanity-check. Fall back to 'per-period, not summarised' rather than guess a single number for an ambiguous row.
features.risk: Medium. Wrong as-of column = wrong headline. Mitigated by explicit as_of_col_header + conservative selection + fall-back-to-omission.

features.name: Unit-aware materiality score + top-N
features.spec: Score every change: materiality = normalized magnitude (abs_delta / line-or-headline scale) x role weight (output >> intermediate >> cosmetic) x downstream reach (graph out-degree to outputs when available). Unit-aware epsilon: percents in pp with a floor, currency in display scale, counts exact. Sort change lists by score; --max keeps top-N by MATERIALITY not first-N by position. Emit a workbook-level 'most material changes' ordering.
features.approach: Compute after format map (+ graph if present). Expose score components so ranking is auditable. Unit-test that a $2m move on Revenue outranks a $2m footnote move and a 3pp churn move outranks a sub-cent recompute.
features.risk: Low-medium. Only affects ordering/truncation, not any single fact's correctness. A bad denominator inverts ranking — normalize against the line's own scale and headline denominators; never silently drop (appendix counts).

features.name: Sheet rename detection + reorder awareness
features.spec: When sheets are BOTH added and removed, fingerprint each (sorted multiset of row labels + sampled values) and pair best matches above a high threshold as renames, then diff as common (avoids the catastrophic whole-sheet add+remove false diff). Record old vs new sheet ORDER; report reorders as a low-severity fact.
features.approach: Greedy stable assignment by descending similarity; only pair when 1:1 best-match is unambiguous and above threshold, else report as genuine add/remove (the safe default). Expose similarity score on the rename fact.
features.risk: Medium. A wrong pairing diffs two unrelated sheets => enormous false structure. Mitigated by high threshold + unambiguous-best-match requirement + visible similarity.

features.name: Robust loader: typed errors + format gate + magic-byte sniff + zip-bomb guard
features.spec: _validate_format() BEFORE load: suffix allowlist {.xlsx,.xlsm} + magic-byte sniff (PK\x03\x04=zip ok; D0CF11E0=OLE2 legacy/encrypted). Wrap both openpyxl loads in try/except (zipfile.BadZipFile, openpyxl InvalidFileException, KeyError, OSError) -> typed WorkbookLoadError. compare() NEVER throws to the agent: on fatal load it returns status='aborted' JSON with diagnostics.errors. Zip-bomb guard: cap uncompressed size / member count before parse. Inputs are UNTRUSTED.
features.approach: On ambiguous zip, attempt load and only error on openpyxl failure (avoid over-rejecting valid mis-packed xlsx). Distinct messages: encrypted vs legacy .xls/.xlsb vs corrupt. cmd_diff relays the typed message verbatim, exits clean.
features.risk: Low. Highest-frequency real-world failure today crashes with a raw traceback. Main risk is over-rejecting a valid mis-named file — magic sniff + attempt-load mitigates.

features.name: Cached-value coverage detection + hard-stop
features.spec: Compute per-sheet coverage = (formula cells with non-None cached value)/(formula cells). Below threshold (~0.5) set LOW_CACHE_COVERAGE warning in diagnostics; the skill MUST hedge that sheet ('source not saved by Excel — alignment low-confidence'). Near-zero coverage across the board => optional hard-stop telling the user to open+save in Excel and re-run.
features.approach: Count during load. Carry the flag into JSON. Skill Stage 2 gates on it.
features.risk: Low. Without it the agent confidently explains alignment noise as real change — directly undermines grounding.

features.name: Populated-set row/col model + hard size/time caps
features.spec: Build rows and cols from the SORTED SET of actually-populated indices (union of content+value keys), NOT dense range(min,max). Cap populated rows/cols per sheet (e.g. 200k rows / 512 cols); on exceed emit SIZE caps_hit and skip structural diff for that sheet (status='degraded'). Budget Translator calls; past threshold degrade --formulas to 'skipped'. --max must bound COMPUTATION not just rendering.
features.approach: Replace range(min_row,max_row+1) with sorted(populated_rows). Move-delta arithmetic uses real row indices, not difflib sequence positions. Caps surfaced prominently so the agent flags the sheet as not-fully-diffed.
features.risk: Low-medium. A stray far cell currently materializes ~1M blank rows and can hang/OOM. Sampling a capped sheet can miss a change — surface the cap so the agent never implies completeness.

features.name: Numeric edge-case hardening + unified epsilon + deterministic move-pairing
features.spec: Guard _round_sig with math.isfinite (return v for NaN/inf); treat Excel error strings (#DIV/0!, #REF!) as literals. Unify the signature rounding with _values_differ tolerance so 'same signature' implies 'not a value change'. Make move-pairing deterministic: sort deleted candidates within each signature/label bucket by row index before popping; tie-break (old_row,new_row). Use fixed decimal quantization to avoid round-half-even run-to-run flips.
features.approach: isfinite guard at top of _round_sig. Single epsilon source feeding both signature and value-diff. Deterministic sort in _match_moves before pop. Regression matrix locks behaviour.
features.risk: Low. A single poisoned cell currently crashes the whole diff. Unifying epsilons could slightly shift which rows align vs flag-as-changed — gate behind golden tests.

features.name: Formula canonicalisation + deshift_ok flag
features.spec: Before comparison, canonicalise: strip insignificant whitespace, uppercase function names, normalise sheet-name quoting and absolute/relative round-trips where semantically equal. Tag each formula fact deshift_ok (Translator succeeded) vs failed; failed => 'advisory/unverified', excluded from asserted logic-change counts. Keep both old_formula and old_formula_canonical so benign re-saves never reach the grounded facts but a real change survives.
features.approach: Conservative provably-equivalent transforms only (whitespace/casing/quoting); leave anything ambiguous as a reported change. Keep raw-string diff as a fallback channel. Token/AST compare DEFERRED.
features.risk: Medium. Over-normalisation could mask a real change (false negative). Mitigated by conservative transforms + keeping raw + tagging canonical-equal-but-raw-differs as cosmetic, not dropped.

features.name: Block / region-move collapsing
features.spec: After per-row move pairing, collapse contiguous runs with constant (old_row->new_row) delta into one block_move fact ('rows 40-51 -> 80-91, +40'). De-noises N single-row moves into one structural fact and gives the narrative a 'section relocated' unit. Fall back to positional/contextual matching for non-unique labels (pair an unmatched insert+delete whose neighbours already matched as a block) instead of requiring global label uniqueness.
features.approach: Require constant delta AND contiguity AND interior rows individually matched before collapsing; leave boundary singletons as single moves.
features.risk: Medium. Aggressive collapse can merge two coincidentally-adjacent independent moves. Mitigated by the constant-delta+contiguity+interior-match requirement. Safe to ship or defer (worst case keeps today's per-row moves).

features.name: Defined-names / named-range diff
features.spec: Load wb.defined_names (workbook + per-sheet scope) from both files; diff added/removed/refers_to-changed/scope-changed and capture the old/new resolved value. Surface named inputs (WACC, Tax_Rate, Churn_Rate) as first-class CAUSES — a WACC/rate change is a headline causal driver. Resolve names when comparing formulas so =B5 -> =WACC reads as a name swap, not an opaque change.
features.approach: Read wb.defined_names; normalise refers_to (absolute/quoting) before comparison to avoid benign churn. Per-sheet scope handling guarded for openpyxl version differences. Name resolution edges into the graph are confidence-tagged.
features.risk: Low-medium. defined_names API differs across openpyxl versions; per-sheet scope is fiddly. Relative/volatile refers_to can show benign churn — normalise first.

features.name: Dependency graph + causal attribution (advisory unless proven)
features.spec: Tokenize formula refs (incl. Sheet!A1, ranges, resolved names) into a cross-sheet precedent/dependent graph. For each cause (input edit/insert/delete/formula rewrite) compute the forward-reachable set restricted to cells that ACTUALLY changed value, intersect with headline outputs -> causal_links edges with method (dep_graph_path vs heuristic_comove), path_proven, and confidence. Split CAUSES from EFFECTS (tier field); tag effects with no upstream cause unexplained:true. The narrative may say 'drove' ONLY for path_proven high-confidence edges; otherwise it must hedge ('likely'/'co-moved with').
features.approach: openpyxl.formula.tokenizer for refs. Reachability is necessity not sufficiency (cached deltas can't prove sufficiency) — label confidence honestly; flag volatile fns (NOW/RAND) and external links as unexplained. Verifier flags any 'caused'/'because' phrasing on a non-high-confidence edge.
features.risk: Medium-high. A wrong causal claim is exactly the hallucination the design forbids. Mitigated by confidence tags + mandatory hedging + verifier phrasing check. Bridge/waterfall contributions DEFERRED (need a re-evaluation engine openpyxl lacks).

features.name: Markdown-injection escaping + utf-8 stdout + path confinement + provenance
features.spec: Escape pipes/backticks/leading #|-| and strip control/bidi chars from any cell text interpolated into tables/inline-code (workbook content must not inject structure into the markdown the agent grounds on). Reconfigure stdout utf-8 errors=replace. Confine -o and --json writes under cwd (reject absolute/.. when skill-driven). Stamp old/new sha256 + diff_run_id into the JSON header; narrative front-matter + verifier echo the same run id.
features.approach: md_escape() applied at every table/inline-code interpolation point in render_markdown. resolve()-under-cwd check on output paths with a clear error. Header provenance fields already in emit_json.
features.risk: Low. Pure hardening; the injection-escape specifically protects the agent's grounding text.

features.name: Mechanical narrative verifier (new module xlsxverify.py)
features.spec: Code that runs AFTER the agent, authoritative. Inputs: narrative MD + diff.json. (1) confirm narrative front-matter diff_run_id == JSON diff_run_id, else REFUSE. (2) extract every citation token {Fnnnn}/{Hnn} and every numeric literal. (3) resolve each token to a fact/headline id; check the adjacent number equals that fact's old_display/new_display/delta_display/raw within format tolerance. (4) FLAG any numeric literal with no nearby citation, any unresolved/dangling id, any number outside the citable set (facts + headline_metrics), and any 'caused'/'because' phrasing on a causal_link whose confidence != high. Output PASS or a list of offending spans. Fail-closed: unparseable => fail.
features.approach: Regex-extract numbers + tokens; conservative tolerance (flag-on-doubt). Its own thorough unit suite. CLI: bizconnect xlsx verify NARRATIVE.md diff.json.
features.risk: Medium. It is THE anti-hallucination control: false-fails annoy, false-passes silently reanimate hallucination risk. Needs conservative tolerances and fail-closed behaviour.

features.name: Tighten skill allowed-tools
features.spec: Narrow SKILL.md allowed-tools from the broad Bash(python *) to the exact launcher pattern (e.g. Bash(python *bizconnect.py xlsx diff *) and the verify invocation) plus Read. Pin/patch openpyxl; avoid pip-install-on-read in the agent path. Note in the skill that it reads arbitrary user-named files.
features.approach: Edit the SKILL.md front-matter allowed-tools list; ensure the pattern still matches the real invocations or the skill breaks.
features.risk: Low. Config-only; the allowed-tools pattern must match the real launcher calls.

skill_orchestration: STAGE 0 — Preflight (code, deterministic): run the diff command which itself validates both paths and runs the format gate (suffix allowlist + magic-byte sniff + zip-bomb guard). The engine NEVER throws — corrupt/encrypted/legacy/.xls inputs come back as status='aborted' JSON with diagnostics.errors. Confine any -o/--json path under cwd.

STAGE 1 — Deterministic diff (code, the ground truth): run `$B xlsx diff OLD NEW --format both --json diff.json -o diff.md --formulas`. Pipeline: populated-set 1D row+column alignment, sheet-rename, named ranges, role classification, dependency graph, headline extraction, materiality scoring, cause/effect tiering. Writes UNCAPPED diff.json (source of truth, stamped diff_run_id + sha256) and a capped diff.md human preview. The agent NEVER re-parses diff.md for figures.

STAGE 2 — Gate on diagnostics (agent reads diff.json header FIRST): if status=='aborted', relay diagnostics.errors[].detail VERBATIM and STOP — do not attempt a narrative or guess. If LOW_CACHE_COVERAGE / caps_hit / LOW_COL_CONFIDENCE present, the narrative MUST open with an explicit hedge banner sourced from those diagnostics; near-zero cache coverage => hard-stop and tell the user to open+save in Excel and re-run. Treat low_confidence_regions as UNCERTAIN structure, not asserted.

STAGE 3 — Routing (agent reads routing.recommend): if total_facts < ~150 AND changed_sheets <= 3 -> INLINE: one grounded pass over the whole diff.json. Else FAN OUT: one subagent per changed sheet (each gets only that sheet's fact slice + global headline_metrics + causal_links for context), or per THEME (revenue/cost/assumptions via role tags) for a single very wide sheet. NEVER silently --max away facts; bulk uncited ripple becomes an appendix COUNT.

STAGE 4 — Grounded authoring (agent/subagents, hard contract): fill the PINNED template — (1) Exec summary 3-5 sentences, figures ONLY from headline_metrics; (2) Headline impact table straight from headline_metrics; (3) What changed grouped by THEME not sheet (Assumptions/inputs, Structure: rows+cols+sheets+named-ranges added/removed/moved, Formula logic), each bullet cited; (4) Causal chains — ONLY edges in causal_links, hedged by confidence ('% Churn 5.0%->2.0% {F0012} drove Revenue +5.5% {H01}' only when path_proven+high, else 'likely'), unexplained effects called out; (5) Caveats/what-to-verify (low-confidence regions, deshift_ok=false advisory formulas, unexplained ripple, low-cache sheets). RULES: a number may appear ONLY if verbatim in some fact's *_display/delta_* or a headline; every quantitative claim carries an inline citation token resolving to a diff.json id; the agent performs ZERO arithmetic. Echo diff_run_id in front-matter.
STAGE 5 — Reduce (only if fanned out): a reducer subagent writes the exec summary from headline_metrics and stitches per-sheet/per-theme sections; it adds NO new figures beyond headline_metrics.

STAGE 6 — Mechanical verification (code, authoritative): run `$B xlsx verify narrative.md diff.json`. Confirms diff_run_id matches; extracts every citation token + numeric literal; resolves each token to a fact/headline and checks the adjacent number within format tolerance; FLAGS uncited numbers, dangling ids, out-of-set numbers, and 'caused'/'because' phrasing on non-high-confidence causal edges. Emits PASS or offending spans. Fail-closed.

STAGE 7 — Repair loop (bounded, max 1-2 iterations): on FAIL, hand the specific offending spans back to the authoring agent to fix ONLY those spans, then re-verify. If still failing after the cap, DOWNGRADE: strip the unverifiable claims and emit with a 'figures below the line are unverified' note — never ship a hallucination, never loop forever.

STAGE 8 — Deliver: present the verified narrative as the primary artifact; link diff.md (human preview) and diff.json (full ground truth + appendix ripple count) so every figure is traceable. Never present an unverified narrative as final.
anti_hallucination: Single source of truth: diff.json is UNCAPPED ground truth with a stable id on every fact; diff.md is only a capped human preview. The agent grounds on diff.json and is forbidden from re-parsing diff.md for figures (the lossy prose-scraping step is what currently invites hallucination).

Closed citable set: a number may appear in the narrative ONLY if it is verbatim in some fact's old_display/new_display/delta_display (or raw) or in the headline_metrics block. Every other figure is out-of-set and the verifier rejects it.

Citation contract: every quantitative claim carries an inline citation token ({Fnnnn}/{Hnn}) that resolves to a diff.json id. The verifier extracts every token and every bare numeric literal; a numeric literal with no nearby resolving citation is a FAIL.

Zero agent arithmetic: all sums, percentages, margins, and deltas are precomputed in code (delta_abs/delta_pct/delta_pp/delta_display, headline_metrics, contributions). The agent quotes them; it never computes. This removes the rounding/transposition surface ('roughly +5%').

Raw vs display split: every fact carries old_raw (exact, for the verifier's tolerance check and for delta math) AND old_display (format-applied, for prose). The agent cites display; the verifier checks against raw within format tolerance, so a transposed or paraphrased digit is caught.

Causation is code-derived, never agent-guessed: causal claims may cite ONLY causal_links edges. The narrative may say 'drove' only for path_proven high-confidence edges; otherwise it must hedge ('likely'/'co-moved with'). The verifier flags 'caused'/'because' phrasing on any non-high-confidence edge. Effects with unexplained:true must be surfaced as unexplained, never auto-attributed to the nearest edit.

Provenance binding: diff.json header carries diff_run_id + old/new sha256; the narrative front-matter echoes diff_run_id; the verifier REFUSES if they differ — preventing a narrative being paired with a JSON from a different run (different files).

Confidence is honest and load-bearing: low cache coverage, low column confidence, signature collisions, and low_confidence_regions are surfaced in diagnostics; the skill gates on them and the narrative hedges or hard-stops rather than confidently explaining alignment noise as real structure.

Fail-closed verifier + bounded repair: the verifier treats unparseable input as FAIL (never fail-open). On failure the skill runs at most 1-2 targeted repair passes, then downgrades by stripping unverifiable claims with a visible note — it never ships an ungrounded narrative and never loops indefinitely.

Markdown-injection containment: cell text interpolated into the report is escaped (pipes/backticks/leading #|-|, control/bidi chars stripped) so a workbook cell containing '| 999 |' or '```' cannot inject structure or a phantom figure into the text the agent grounds on.
tests: Golden byte-stability: compare(A,B) emits byte-identical diff.json across 5 runs (deterministic id assignment, sorted move-pairing, fixed decimal quantization) — guards the verifier against false-fails.

Column-insert regression (the core fix): synthetic 200-row x 10-year model, insert one FY column -> assert EXACTLY one columns.inserted fact and ZERO spurious value/input facts (the column-axis analogue of the 16,300 failure).

Pure row insert -> 1 inserted fact, 0 spurious cell changes. Pure column insert -> 1 column-inserted fact, 0 spurious changes AND row alignment NOT degraded (proves the column-shift-invariant row signature).

Simultaneous row+col insert -> independent 1D passes recover both facts; if col_confidence low, assert the sheet is flagged uncertain (LOW_COL_CONFIDENCE) and falls back to row-only rather than emitting a wall of bogus changes (joint 2D explicitly deferred).
_round_sig hardening: feed inf, -inf, NaN, -0.0, 1e308, 1e-308, '#DIV/0!', '#REF!' through signature()/_round_sig/_values_differ -> no exception; error strings compared as literals.

Robust-loader matrix: corrupt zip, truncated download, encrypted .xlsx (OLE2 magic), real .xls, real .xlsb, mis-named .csv, empty file -> each yields status='aborted' + the correct typed message, ZERO tracebacks; cmd_diff exits clean.
Cached-value coverage: library-written workbook with all-None cached values -> LOW_CACHE_COVERAGE in diagnostics; near-zero triggers the hard-stop path.

Sparse/large: cell at row 1 + cell at row 1,048,576 only -> populated-set model completes fast (assert bounded wall-clock/memory, no 1M-row materialization); >cap sheet -> SIZE caps_hit recorded, structural diff skipped, no hang.

Number-format/unit: 0.05->0.02 with '0.0%' -> display 5.0%/2.0%, unit_kind percent, delta -3.0pp; 5.5e6 with $'000 scale -> $5.5m; counts stay integer; unknown custom format -> raw + unit_kind=unknown (no wrong-unit assertion); assert old_raw preserved exactly.

Row-role + materiality: Revenue/EBITDA/Total rows tagged correctly with confidence; unconventional labels degrade to omission not misclassification; a $2m Revenue move outranks a $2m footnote move; a 3pp churn move outranks a sub-cent recompute; --max keeps top-N by materiality not position.

Headline extraction (GM-shaped fixture): seed Revenue/EBITDA/margin rows with the GM deltas -> assert headline_metrics contains Revenue +5.5%, EBITDA +12%, margin 8.06%->8.55% with correct units (pp vs $m) and source_fact_ids pointing to real cells.

Causal attribution: % Churn cause feeds Revenue & EBITDA via the formula graph -> causal_links[C].effect_headline_ids == [H01,H02] with path_proven=true; an unrelated edit does NOT link; an external-link/volatile cell with no precedent -> unexplained:true; heuristic_comove edges carry method+low/medium confidence.

Sheet rename: identical content under 'Model v3'->'Model v4' (similarity 0.94) -> one renamed_sheets fact + diffed as common, NOT whole-sheet add+remove; a 2-add/2-remove fixture with one true rename -> only that pair links, rest stay add/remove.
Named-range diff: Churn_Rate value/refers_to change and a formula =B5 -> =WACC -> surfaced as a named_ranges entry + a name swap, treated as a headline causal input.

Block-move collapse: 12-row Opex section relocated with constant delta (with and without intra-block ripple, with repeated 'Total' labels) -> exactly one blocks_moved fact; boundary singletons remain single moves.

Formula canonicalisation: whitespace/casing/quoting/abs-rel benign re-save -> 0 logic changes (logic_changed=false); a real argument change -> logic_changed=true with tokens_changed identifying WHICH part; a deshift failure -> deshift_ok=false tagged advisory, not a confident change.
Markdown-injection: cell text '| 999 |' / '```' / '# Total' -> escaped in diff.md and the verifier still parses cleanly; no phantom figure injected.

JSON schema contract: validate diff.json (schema_version present, ids unique, every effect's explained_by resolves, every headline source_fact_id resolves, diff_run_id + sha256 present); snapshot-stability test.

Verifier unit tests: (a) narrative citing a correct {H01} +5.5% PASSES; (b) fabricated 'EBITDA up ~15%' with no matching headline FAILS with the offending span; (c) uncited bare number FAILS; (d) dangling {F9999} FAILS; (e) diff_run_id mismatch REFUSED; (f) 'churn CAUSED Revenue' on a low-confidence edge FAILS; (g) unparseable input FAILS (fail-closed).

Skill-flow + repair: <150-fact 2-sheet diff routes inline; GM-scale routes fanout_per_sheet with no facts silently dropped (appendix count == un-narrated effect facts); low cache coverage triggers hedge/hard-stop; a seeded fabricated figure triggers exactly one repair pass then PASSES; an unfixable claim triggers graceful downgrade (stripped + noted), never ships.

End-to-end GM acceptance: the ~16,300-naive-change model -> verified narrative leads with Revenue +5.5% / EBITDA +12% / margin 8.06%->8.55%, attributes them to % Churn 0.05->0.02 and the named structural edits, flags unexplained ripple, and PASSES the verifier with zero uncited numbers; totals show ~6 moves/6 inserts/4 deletes/20 inputs.
cut_or_defer: DEFER: Joint/iterative 2D fixed-point alignment. We ship INDEPENDENT 1D row and column passes (with a column-shift-invariant row signature) plus row_confidence/col_confidence and a row-only fallback. Justification: the joint fixed-point is the single highest-complexity feature; it can fail to converge or mis-anchor on label-collision-heavy sheets, and a wrong column anchor cascades worse than a wrong row anchor. The 1D passes already kill the column-axis 16,300 noise (the actual user pain); joint solving is a refinement, not the core promise. Revisit once the 1D passes have golden coverage and we have a label-collision benchmark.

DEFER: Full global cross-sheet reference-shift suppression (coordinate edit-script remap of all Sheet!Ref formulas). Justification: it is the correct generalisation but a remap off-by-one in ONE sheet's script silently SUPPRESSES real changes across every cross-sheet formula referencing it — worse than over-reporting. For v1, keep per-sheet de-shift, isolate --formulas per sheet, count deshift_failures, and mark cross-sheet formula changes 'advisory' in diagnostics so the narrative hedges. Land it only after the per-sheet edit-script is proven and tested on a 2-sheet cross-reference fixture.

DEFER: Per-headline bridge / waterfall decomposition (signed contributions reconciling old_total->new_total). Justification: true attribution needs a formula re-evaluation engine openpyxl does NOT provide; contributions from cached values alone are approximate and a confidently-wrong CFO waterfall is the single most misleading possible output. Ship the causal_links (which output moved, hedged by confidence) without claiming the split. Revisit only with an honest residual term and high graph confidence.

DOWNGRADE to advisory-only for v1: the dependency graph causal links. They ship (SHOULD) but every edge is confidence-tagged and the narrative MUST hedge ('likely'/'co-moved') unless path_proven+high; the verifier blocks 'caused'/'because' phrasing on weak edges. A proven graph path is the only thing that earns the word 'drove'.

CUT for v1: Merged-cell geometry diff and array/dynamic-array .ref extent comparison (COULD). Justification: the non-read_only merge pass is heavy and these affect far fewer financial models than the 2D/units fixes. Keep only the cheap safety guard: a value-present/content-empty cell (spill result) must NEVER be classified as an INPUT change. Gate the full merge/array work behind a flag for a later release.

CUT for v1: Token/AST-level formula comparison. Ship only conservative string canonicalisation (whitespace/casing/quoting/abs-rel). Justification: a token parser bug that masks a real change is a false negative feeding the grounded narrative; the conservative canonicalisation captures the high-frequency benign-re-save noise without that risk. tokens_changed can be coarse (range/argument granularity) until an AST compare is justified.
KEEP minimal, do not gold-plate: sheet-reorder reporting is emitted as a low-severity fact only; it never affects alignment or headlines.
